selected_important_turn.text: I need 3 food packages as it is required to feed children who visited unexpectedly. Instead you can take water and firewood
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is an early, high-leverage claim: the opponent opened aggressively and YOU replied by matching with an exclusive claim to all food. That exchange set a zero-sum tone and narrowed options for joint value creation early in the negotiation.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The turn is rigid and competitive (claims all food) and ignores the opponent's expressed needs (they also asked for food and water, mentioned children). It forecloses integrative moves (tradeoffs, conditional concessions) and provokes resistance.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from a pure demand to a problem-solving posture: acknowledge the opponent's children/needs, state your minimum but invite tradeoffs or ask about their flexibility (e.g., 'I need food for my children — what parts of water/firewood could you be flexible on?'). Offer a conditional trade (I can take 2 food if you take X) rather than an absolute take-all demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — opening space for tradeoffs and conditional offers will create more feasible splits instead of zero-sum fighting.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — acknowledging the other's needs and using conditional concessions tends to produce more balanced outcomes.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — less adversarial language reduces resistance and speeds convergence.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — showing empathy and asking for options reduces hostility and builds trust.

value2reason.Low: I need 1 extra firewood package because I don't want to run out if our unexpected guests stay up longer.
value2reason.Medium: I need 3 additional water packages because we had some unexpected guest join us camping at the last minute.
value2reason.High: I need 3 extra food packages because a few unexoected guest decide to come camping with us last minute.

value2reason.Low: I would prefer firewood to make food myself.
value2reason.Medium: Water is also necessary as much as food to live.
value2reason.High: Food is basic necessity for living long. More than other things Food is the most needed thing to survive.

dialogue.text: Hi'
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hello I would like to take 3 extra food packages and 3 extra water package and 1 extra firewood packages because we had some unexpected guest join us on our camping trip.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 3 food packages as it is required to feed children who visited unexpectedly. Instead you can take water and firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need food for my children — what parts of water or firewood could you be flexible on? I'm willing to trade some of my food for your water or firewood if it helps us both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need 3 food packages for the unexpected guests. I'm willing to trade 1 food for 2 water or 1 firewood if it helps balance things. What do you need most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 3 food packages for my children, but I'm open to trading 1 food for 2 water or 1 firewood if it helps us split fairly. What’s your minimum for water or firewood?
